LOCAL GUIDE

How to Choose the Best Financial Advisor in Lehi, Utah (2026)

A comprehensive guide to finding the right financial advisor for your retirement planning needs.

12 min read Updated 2026

Finding the Right Advisor Matters

Choosing a financial advisor is one of the most important financial decisions you'll make. The right advisor can add significant value to your retirement plan. The wrong one can cost you hundreds of thousands of dollars in unnecessary fees, taxes, and missed opportunities.

Lehi, Utah, sits at the heart of Silicon Slopes and has grown rapidly over the past decade. With that growth comes a proliferation of financial advisors and firms. This guide helps you navigate the landscape and find the right fit for your retirement planning needs.

What to Look for in a Financial Advisor

1. Fiduciary Standard

A fiduciary advisor is legally obligated to act in your best interest. This is the highest standard of care in financial advice. Not all advisors are fiduciaries — many operate under a lesser "suitability" standard.

Questions to ask:

  • Are you a fiduciary at all times?
  • Will you put that in writing?
  • Do you receive commissions from any products?

2. Specialization

Financial planning is broad. The best advisor for your retirement is one who specializes in retirement income planning, not a generalist who handles everything from college savings to business insurance.

Questions to ask:

  • What percentage of your clients are retirees?
  • Do you specialize in retirement income planning?
  • What tax planning strategies do you use?

3. Fee Structure

Understand how your advisor gets paid. Fee-only advisors charge clients directly and don't earn commissions. Fee-based advisors may charge fees AND earn commissions, creating potential conflicts.

Questions to ask:

  • Are you fee-only or fee-based?
  • What is your total cost to me annually?
  • Are there any hidden or additional fees?

4. Experience & Track Record

Look for advisors with significant experience, particularly with clients in your financial situation. Years in business, client retention, and community involvement all matter.

Questions to ask:

  • How long have you been advising clients?
  • What's your client retention rate?
  • Can you describe your planning process?

Utah-Specific Retirement Planning Considerations

Utah Tax Landscape

Utah has a flat 4.55% state income tax. Understanding how this interacts with federal tax planning is essential for Utah retirees. The state retirement credit can also reduce your tax burden.

Cost of Living

Utah County's cost of living has risen significantly. Your retirement plan needs to account for local housing costs, healthcare expenses, and inflation specific to the Wasatch Front.

Local Expertise

An advisor familiar with Utah's regulatory environment, local tax laws, and community resources provides added value over a remote advisor unfamiliar with the area.

Red Flags to Watch For

  • Guaranteeing specific investment returns
  • Pressuring you to make quick decisions
  • Refusing to disclose their fee structure
  • Recommending products from their own company
  • Not asking detailed questions about your situation
  • Offering a one-size-fits-all plan

Green Flags

  • Fiduciary commitment in writing
  • Transparent, understandable fee structure
  • Thorough discovery process before recommendations
  • Specialization in your specific needs
  • Strong community reputation and involvement
  • Regular reviews and proactive communication

Steps to Choose Your Advisor

  1. Define Your Needs — Know what you need help with: retirement income, tax planning, estate planning, or comprehensive management.
  2. Research Options — Look for fiduciary advisors in Lehi and Utah County. Check credentials, reviews, and regulatory records.
  3. Interview 2–3 Advisors — Meet with multiple advisors. Ask about their process, fees, specialization, and how they would approach your situation.
  4. Make Your Decision — Choose the advisor who understands your situation, communicates clearly, and operates as a fiduciary.

About Capital Wealth

Capital Wealth is a fiduciary financial advisor in Lehi, Utah serving families since 2010. We specialize in retirement income planning with a fee-only compensation model — no commissions, no conflicts.

What we offer:

  • Proprietary Retirement Money Map process
  • Tax-efficient retirement strategies
  • Social Security optimization
  • Medicare and IRMAA planning
  • Estate and legacy planning
  • Federal employee benefits expertise
